branding.title: Discover My Personal Brand

branding.title: Turn My Knowledge Into Content

branding.title: Build an Engaged Community

branding.title: Build Authority in My Industry

branding.title: Create Educational Content

interview.title: Create My Interview Prep Plan
interview.text: I have an interview for the role of [Job Title] at [Company] in [X] days. Create a personalized interview preparation plan with daily goals, technical topics, behavioral questions, mock interviews, company research tasks, and confidence-building exercises.

interview.title: Analyze My Resume Like a Recruiter
interview.text: Review my resume [paste resume]. Identify weak areas, missing achievements, unclear bullet points, and ATS issues. Then predict the interview questions a recruiter is most likely to ask based on my resume.

interview.title: Research the Company Thoroughly
interview.text: I have an interview with [Company]. Create a detailed company research brief covering its business model, products, competitors, recent news, financial performance, culture, leadership, and likely interview questions related to the company.

interview.title: Generate Role-Specific Questions
interview.text: I’m interviewing for a [Job Title] position with [X] years of experience. Generate 100 realistic interview questions covering technical knowledge, behavioral scenarios, problem-solving, and situational judgment. Organize them by difficulty.

interview.title: Conduct a Mock Interview
interview.text: Act as an experienced interviewer for a [Job Title] role. Ask me one interview question at a time, wait for my answer, evaluate it like a hiring manager, suggest improvements, and then move to the next question.

interview.title: Improve My Interview Answers
interview.text: I'll paste my answer to an interview question. Rewrite it using the STAR method, improve clarity, highlight measurable achievements, and make it sound confident, professional, and authentic.

interview.title: Identify My Weak Areas
interview.text: Based on my resume, experience, and target role, identify the skills or knowledge gaps that may reduce my interview performance. Create a focused improvement plan to address them before the interview.

interview.title: Master Behavioral Interviews
interview.text: Teach me how to answer behavioral interview questions using the STAR method. Generate 30 common behavioral questions and provide examples of strong, structured answers.

interview.title: Prepare for Technical Interviews
interview.text: I’m interviewing for a [Job Title] role. Create a technical interview preparation roadmap covering key concepts, hands-on exercises, coding challenges if applicable, and practical scenarios likely to appear.

interview.title: Prepare for Case Study Questions
interview.text: Generate realistic case studies and scenario-based interview questions for my target role. After I answer, evaluate my reasoning, communication, and decision-making.

interview.title: Improve My Communication
interview.text: Evaluate how I communicate during interviews. Help me improve my confidence, clarity, tone, body language, storytelling, and ability to answer concisely.

interview.title: Build Strong Self-Introduction
interview.text: Help me create a powerful ‘Tell me about yourself’ answer tailored to my background, achievements, and the job I'm applying for. Keep it engaging, professional, and under 2 minutes.

interview.title: Prepare for Salary Negotiation
interview.text: Teach me how to confidently negotiate salary after receiving a job offer. Include preparation steps, negotiation scripts, common mistakes, and responses to employer objections.

interview.title: Create an Interview Cheat Sheet
interview.text: Based on my target role, prepare a one-page interview revision sheet with key concepts, formulas, frameworks, achievements, questions to ask the interviewer, and last-minute reminders.

interview.title: Answer Tough Interview Questions
interview.text: Generate difficult interview questions such as employment gaps, career changes, failures, weaknesses, conflicts, layoffs, and low grades. Help me answer them honestly and professionally.

interview.title: Build Interview Confidence
interview.text: I get nervous before interviews. Create a 7-day confidence-building routine with mock interviews, breathing exercises, visualization, and communication drills.

interview.title: Prepare Follow-Up Communication
interview.text: Help me write professional thank-you emails, follow-up emails, and response templates after interviews. Explain when each should be sent and what they should include.

interview.title: Prepare for HR & Panel Interviews
interview.text: Explain the differences between HR, hiring manager, technical, and panel interviews. Create preparation strategies and sample questions for each interview stage.

interview.title: Analyze My Interview Performance
interview.text: After every interview, I'll share the questions I was asked and my answers. Analyze my performance, identify mistakes, suggest stronger responses, and create an improvement plan for the next interview.

interview.title: Build a 30-Day Mastery Plan
interview.text: I want to become interview-ready within 30 days. Create a complete day-by-day preparation schedule including technical learning, behavioral practice, mock interviews, resume review, company research, and confidence-building.

interview.title: Become My Personal Interview Coach
interview.text: Act as a senior interviewer with 20+ years of hiring experience. Continuously coach me throughout my interview preparation by challenging my answers, identifying blind spots, improving my communication, and ensuring I become one of the strongest candidates.

learning.title: Learn By Doing (Hands-on Mentor)
learning.text:
Act as a hands-on mentor who teaches me by building real things.

Here is how I want you to help me:

1️⃣ Ask me what I want to build and my target role as [TARGET ROLE].

2️⃣ Break the project into small, real-world tasks.
• Beginner-friendly
• Job-relevant
• Incremental difficulty

3️⃣ For each task:
• Explain only what I need to complete it
• Show one real-world use case
• Avoid theory overload

4️⃣ Ask me to attempt the task first.
• Let me struggle a bit
• Do not give the full solution immediately

5️⃣ If I get stuck:
• Give hints step by step
• Reveal the solution only at the end

6️⃣ After each task:
• Explain what skill I just learned
• How it maps to real jobs

7️⃣ End with a reflection.
• What I built
• What improved
• What to build next

*Image Prompt* 👉Analyze the uploaded storyboard image carefully.

Your task is NOT to generate an image.
Your task is NOT to create a new storyboard.
Your task is to act as a professional commercial director and convert the uploaded storyboard image into a complete Google Flow video prompt.

The final output must be only a ready-to-use cinematic video prompt for Google Flow.

STEP 1 — STORYBOARD ANALYSIS

Carefully analyze the uploaded storyboard image:

* Main subject or product
* Number of storyboard panels
* Camera angles
* Character actions
* Product interactions
* Facial expressions
* Emotions
* Lighting style
* Environment
* Visual progression
* Beginning, middle, and ending
* Final commercial payoff

Identify the exact sequence from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.

Do not skip any storyboard panel.

Treat every panel as a separate video scene.

STEP 2 — VIDEO STRUCTURE

Create a complete Google Flow video prompt using:

Duration: 10 seconds
Aspect Ratio: 9:16
Style: Ultra photorealistic, cinematic, high-end commercial production
Quality: Premium advertising quality, realistic lighting, realistic shadows, shallow depth of field, professional camera movement
Continuity: Keep the same character, same product, same clothes, same colors, same accessories, same hairstyle, same face, and same visual identity throughout the video.

STEP 3 — SCENE BREAKDOWN

Convert every storyboard panel into a video scene.

For every scene include:

Scene 1

* What is happening
* Camera movement
* Subject action
* Product interaction
* Emotion
* Lighting
* Transition

Scene 2

* What is happening
* Camera movement
* Subject action
* Product interaction
* Emotion
* Lighting
* Transition

Scene 3

* What is happening
* Camera movement
* Subject action
* Product interaction
* Emotion
* Lighting
* Transition

Continue until all storyboard panels are converted into scenes.

STEP 4 — VOICE RECORD / DIALOGUE

Add one short Hindi voice dialogue during the first 2 seconds.

Requirements:

* Natural Indian Hindi
* 2 to 6 words only
* Should match the emotion of the video
* Should sound like a real person speaking
* Mention it as voice-over or spoken dialogue
* No subtitles
* No captions
* No on-screen text

Example format:

Voice-over / Dialogue 0–2 sec: “Yahi hai asli freshness!”

STEP 5 — SOUND DESIGN

Add realistic sound effects according to the storyboard.

Include:

* Product handling sound
* Can opening / bottle opening sound if product is a drink
* Fizz sound if beverage is shown
* Splash sound if liquid appears
* Natural ambience
* Smooth transition sound
* Final cinematic hit or soft commercial ending sound

STEP 6 — MUSIC

Automatically choose background music based on the storyboard category:

Beauty → Luxury beauty music
Food or drink → Energetic commercial music
Tech → Modern commercial music
Fashion → Trendy music
Jewellery → Elegant luxury music
Lifestyle → Uplifting commercial music
Product ad → Premium commercial music

STEP 7 — FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT

Output only the final Google Flow video prompt.

Do not explain.
Do not analyze separately.
Do not give notes.
Do not give suggestions.
Do not create an image prompt.
Do not mention “create image.”
Do not write storyboard generation instructions.
Do not add extra captions or text.

FINAL FLOW VIDEO PROMPT FORMAT:

Use the uploaded storyboard image as the exact visual reference and starting guide. Create a 10-second vertical 9:16 cinematic video based on the storyboard sequence.

Music:

Sound Effects:

Style:

Consistency Rules:
